steps.title: Serum from cattle persistently infected with...
steps.instruction: Serum from cattle persistently infected with BVDV (BVDV-1b strain Shihoro/B_6, Hirose et al. [17]) Reagents

steps.title: Anti-pestiviral NS3 antibody (prepared in-house, see Kameyama et al
steps.instruction: Anti-pestiviral NS3 antibody (prepared in-house, see Kameyama et al. [18])

steps.title: Primer sets used for CPER of...
steps.instruction: Primer sets used for CPER of BVDV-1b Fragment Genome size (bp) Orientation Nucleotide sequence (5′–3′) BVDV-1b Fragment 1 1303 Forward GTATACGAGGTTAGGCAAGTTC Reverse GATTTTCTCTGGCCAGATC BVDV-1b Fragment 2 2401 Forward GATCTGGCCAGAGAAAATC Reverse CTCTCTTAGTAGTAGGTATAGTAG BVDV-1b Fragment 3 2316 Forward CTACTATACCTACTACTAAGAGAG Reverse CATGTATTGATAGACTGACTCAGCTGC BVDV-1b Fragment 4 2501 Forward GCAGCTGAGTCAGTCTATCAATACATG Reverse CTTATCTTCCCTTCAGAGTCCATC BVDV-1b Fragment 5 2236 Forward GATGGACTCTGAAGGGAAGATAAG Reverse GTGCTTCTCTGAGTCCAGTAC BVDV-1b Fragment 6 1652 Forward GTACTGGACTCAGAGAAGCAC Reverse GGGGCTGTTAAGGGTTTTCCCTAGTC BVDV-1b Linker fragment 738 Forward ACCCTTAACAGCCCCCCCCCCCAACTTCGGAGGTCGAC Reverse GAACTTGCCTAACCTCGTATACAATAACCCGGCGGCCCAAAATGC Solutions

steps.title: Maintenance medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Maintenance medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume DMEM Not applicable 500 mL BVDV antibody-free FBS 9.1% 50 mL Penicillin-streptomycin 1% 5 mL

steps.title: 2% FBS medium Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 2% FBS medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or volume DMEM Not applicable 500 mL BVDV antibody-free FBS 2% 10 mL Penicillin-streptomycin 1% 5 mL Laboratory supplies

steps.title: Follow the manufacturer’s protocol to purify...
steps.instruction: Follow the manufacturer’s protocol to purify total RNA from the serum of cattle persistently infected with BVDV using the PureLink TM RNA Mini kit (see General note 1). Use 100 μL of serum stored at -80 °C. B. Generation of viral cDNA fragments

steps.title: Reverse transcribe total RNA into cDNA...
steps.instruction: Reverse transcribe total RNA into cDNA using the SuperScript TM IV VILO TM master mix. Mix 16 μL of total RNA sample with 4 μL of SuperScript TM IV VILO TM master mix and heat at 25 °C for 10 min, 50 °C for 10 min, and 85 °C for 5 min.

steps.title: Reaction mixture Reagent Volume cDNA 1...
steps.instruction: Reaction mixture Reagent Volume cDNA 1 μL Forward primer (10 μM) 1.5 μL Reverse primer (10 μM) 1.5 μL KOD One ® PCR master mix 25 μL H 2 O 21 μL Table

steps.title: Assess the size of the fragments...
steps.instruction: Assess the size of the fragments by gel electrophoresis using a commercial 1 kb DNA ladder as a reference.

steps.title: Follow the manufacturer’s protocol to purify...
steps.instruction: Follow the manufacturer’s protocol to purify the PCR fragments using the FastGene Gel/PCR Extraction kit.

steps.title: Measure DNA concentration using NanoDrop
steps.instruction: Measure DNA concentration using NanoDrop.

steps.title: Because CPER is based on assembly...
steps.instruction: Because CPER is based on assembly PCR, the correct joining of fragments via the overlapping regions is critical for making the complete circular cDNA assembly. Thus, use PCR assembly to connect neighboring fragments by PrimeSTAR ® GXL DNA polymerase (see Troubleshooting 1). C. Circular polymerase extension reaction (CPER)

steps.title: Mix each gene fragment of BVDV...
steps.instruction: Mix each gene fragment of BVDV cDNA and the linker fragment at 0.1 pmol per fragment.

steps.title: Assemble all fragments of equal molar...
steps.instruction: Assemble all fragments of equal molar amounts using PrimeSTAR ® GXL DNA polymerase (the recipe of this reaction mixture is in Table 4 ; thermocycling conditions for the CPER are detailed in Table 5 ). Table

steps.title: CPER mixture Reagent Volume Each gene...
steps.instruction: CPER mixture Reagent Volume Each gene fragment of BVDV cDNA and the linker fragment 0.1 pmol per fragment dNTP mixture 4 μL 5× PrimeSTAR GXL buffer 10 μL PrimeSTAR GXL DNA polymerase 1 μL H 2 O Up to 50 μL Table

steps.title: Prepare cells
steps.instruction: Prepare cells. a. One day before transfection, co-culture MDBK and 293T cells in a 2:1 ratio (see General note

steps.title: Immediately after the CPER, add 25...
steps.instruction: Immediately after the CPER, add 25 μL of CPER product to 200 μL of Opti-MEM warmed to 37 °C and mix by pipetting.

steps.title: Add 12 μL of TransIT-LT1 ®...
steps.instruction: Add 12 μL of TransIT-LT1 ® transfection reagent and mix by slow pipetting.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 5,000× g for several...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 5,000× g for several seconds and incubate for 10 min.

steps.title: Add the whole mixture to the...
steps.instruction: Add the whole mixture to the cells and culture in a CO 2 incubator at 37 °C in a humidified atmosphere with 5% CO 2 .

steps.title: After 4 days of transfection, collect...
steps.instruction: After 4 days of transfection, collect 100 μL of culture supernatant in a flat bottom microtube and store at -80 °C. E. Detection of viruses by immunofluorescence staining

steps.title: Inoculate newly seeded MDBK cells at...
steps.instruction: Inoculate newly seeded MDBK cells at 80% confluence in a collagen-coated 24-well microplate with 100 μL of culture supernatant.

steps.title: After 5 days of infection, wash...
steps.instruction: After 5 days of infection, wash with D-PBS and fix cells with acetone for 10 min at 4 °C. After washing with D-PBS three times, the plate can be stored at 4 °C.

steps.title: Incubate with the indicated anti-pestiviral NS3...
steps.instruction: Incubate with the indicated anti-pestiviral NS3 antibody (1:1,000 dilution) in D-PBS for 1 h at 37 °C in a humidified atmosphere.

steps.title: After washing with D-PBS three times,...
steps.instruction: After washing with D-PBS three times, incubate cells in the dark with goat anti-mouse IgG Alexa Fluor 488-conjugated (1:1,000 dilution) secondary antibody in D-PBS for 1 h at room temperature.

steps.title: After washing with D-PBS four times,...
steps.instruction: After washing with D-PBS four times, observe under the fluorescence microscope using the BX-Z filter GFP ( Figure 1 ). Figure

steps.title: Image of immunofluorescence staining showing bovine...
steps.instruction: Image of immunofluorescence staining showing bovine viral diarrhea virus (BVDV)-positive cells. Antiviral NS3 immunofluorescence image of infected MDBK cells (A) and non-infectious condition (B). Scale bar, 100 μm.

description: Validation of protocol This protocol, or parts of it, have been applied and validated in the following research article: • Tamura et al. [13]. A rapid and versatile reverse genetics approach for generating recombinant positive-strand RNA viruses that use IRES-mediated translation (see Figure 2B , H, I).
